Debris from an intercepted drone caused a fire in the Fujairah crude loading terminal area (Fujairah Oil Industry Zone), forcing a temporary suspension of some oil-loading operations while authorities contained the blaze; no casualties were reported and the disruption appears localized rather than a full shutdown.

Additional Conditional Formatting
Partial Shut-in
Additional Conditional Formatting
Rumaila South shut in, which contributes 700 kbpd of supply, due to lower storage availability on the 3rd of March. Two drones landed on the field on 5th March, leading to BP evacuating foreign staff from the field. Subsqequnet cuts were implemented over the days. Additional cuts of 100 kbpd implemented on Rumaila North from 24th of March.

Caribbean offshore: Emergence of a globally significant oil and gas hub
Over the past 10 years, the offshore Caribbean has emerged as a global exploration hotspot. Over the next 10 years, the region is forecast to become a key source of global oil and gas supply.

Algeria amends constitution, further centralizing power, reinforcing executive control and limiting opposition ahead of June 2026 elections
Algeria’s parliament unanimously approved amendments to the 2020 constitution on March 25, adding to previous administrative and political reforms affecting the June 2026 parliamentary elections.
